compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Finding

`src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py`'s `verify_findings()` is the shared Pass-2 kernel
entry point that chunks findings, runs each chunk via an injected `run_chunk` LLM callable, and
tallies `outcome_counts` (`clean`/`recovered`/`empty_outcomes`/`unrecoverable`) plus
`contract_violations`. It is fully implemented and unit-tested
(`tests/unit/test_review_kernel.py`, `tests/unit/test_review_kernel_rules.py`), but neither live
consumer pipeline calls it, and — per plan-review feedback on an earlier draft of this ticket —
neither CAN call it as-is: both pipelines run their Pass-2 model call as a declarative workflow
prompt step (one YAML step per token-budgeted chunk, merged by the executor), not as a
procedural loop with a `run_chunk` callback in scope. `verify_findings()`'s per-chunk
`outcome_counts` tally (which chunk was clean vs empty vs a `StructuredOutputError`) depends on
being IN that per-chunk loop; by the time `plan_review_decide`/`code_review_decide` see the
input, the workflow engine has already merged every chunk's output into one flat
`verifications` list, and per-chunk boundaries (which chunk contributed what) are gone. So
`empty_outcomes`/`unrecoverable` (chunk-level execution signals) are **not reconstructable** at
the `*_decide` step boundary without a separate architecture change (the verify prompt step
itself emitting per-chunk metadata as a named workflow output before the merge) — that is
explicitly deferred as follow-on work, not this ticket's scope.

What IS shared today, and inconsistently, between the two pipelines:

- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py`'s `plan_review_decide` (~line 425) calls
  `review_kernel.reshape_verifications(...)` on the merged `verifications` list, and on
  `reshape.has_violations` (malformed/duplicate/unexpected indices) calls
  `orchestrator.record_contract_violation(reshape.summary())` — a run-scoped ContextVar sink
  (`plan_review/orchestrator.py:321-357`: `collect_contract_violations`/
  `record_contract_violation`/`drain_contract_violations`) activated in
  `gate_dispatch.produce_plan_review_verdict` (~line 171) and drained into
  `coverage["verification_contract_violations"]` in `plan_review_coach` (~line 563).
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py`'s `code_review_decide` (~line 395) does the exact
  same `reshape_verifications(...)` call and `has_violations` check, but only
  `logger.error(...)`s — no sink exists for code-review, so the violation data never reaches
  `finalize_code_review_verdict`'s coverage, and there is no `outcome_counts` telemetry there at
  all.

## Scope note

`c2c5-53a7-2ce0-4d98` is a child of epic `6eca-183e-1cd2-4fb9` (Janitor Cleanup 2026-07-19),
discovered mid-run (`discovered_from` -> `1aa8-cdde-4ff6-40d0`) after the epic's other 5 leaves
had already been decomposed and were not in the epic's original plan/file_impact. It was left
open and unworked when the other 5 leaves closed, per an explicit scoping decision recorded on
the epic. It is now being completed under direct operator instruction ("Complete this story"),
which supersedes that earlier deferral.

## Remediation

Two concrete, single-path changes (no `run_chunk`/`verify_findings()` involvement — that
kernel entry point stays reserved for callers that own the procedural chunk loop, e.g. a
future non-workflow consumer):

1. **Shared contract-violation sink.** Move the existing ContextVar-backed sink
   (`collect_contract_violations`/`record_contract_violation`/`drain_contract_violations`,
   currently private to `plan_review/orchestrator.py`) into `review_kernel` (e.g.
   `review_kernel/verify.py` or a new `review_kernel/telemetry.py`) as the ONE implementation
   both pipelines import — not a duplicate. Update `plan_review/orchestrator.py`'s and
   `plan_review/workflow_ops.py`'s existing call sites to import it from its new home (a
   mechanical rename, no behavior change for plan-review). Wire the equivalent into
   `code_review`: activate `collect_contract_violations()` around the workflow run in
   `gate_dispatch._run_code_review_gate` (mirroring `produce_plan_review_verdict`'s
   `with assemble_context_cache(), collect_contract_violations(), gate_config(cfg):`), call
   `record_contract_violation(reshape.summary())` in `code_review_decide` when
   `reshape.has_violations` (replacing the bare `logger.error`, keeping the log too), and drain
   it into `finalize_code_review_verdict`'s verdict coverage as
   `coverage["verification_contract_violations"]`, mirroring plan-review's
   `coverage["verification_contract_violations"]` write in `plan_review_coach`.

2. **`outcome_counts` at the `*_decide` boundary (whole-run tally, not per-chunk).** Add a
   small shared helper in `review_kernel` — e.g.
   `decide_outcome_counts(raw_verifs: list, findings: list, reshape: VerificationReshape) ->
   dict[str, int]` — that derives a single-run `{"clean": int, "recovered": int,
   "empty_outcomes": int, "unrecoverable": int}` from data already available at the merged
   `*_decide` boundary:
   - `empty_outcomes = 1` if `findings` is non-empty but `raw_verifs` came back completely
     empty, else `0`.
   - `unrecoverable` is **always `0`** in this leaf — a `StructuredOutputError` inside one
     chunk is swallowed by the workflow engine's own repair/retry layer before the merged
     output ever reaches `*_decide`; a total verify-step failure already surfaces via the
     existing `coverage["verify_failed"]` signal (ticket 59bc/b24d), which this ticket does not
     touch. Tracking true per-chunk `unrecoverable` counts requires the verify prompt step
     itself to emit per-chunk metadata as a named workflow output before the merge — an
     explicitly deferred follow-on, not this ticket.
   - Otherwise (findings present and at least one verification came back):
     `recovered = 1, clean = 0` if `reshape.has_violations` (some entries were malformed/
     duplicate/unexpected but the run still produced usable verifications), else
     `clean = 1, recovered = 0`.
   Call this helper from both `plan_review_decide` and `code_review_decide` and add
   `outcome_counts` to each step's output dict (additive — no existing output key removed).
   Thread it into each pipeline's verdict coverage the same way `hierarchy_incomplete` was
   threaded in ticket b24d: `coverage["outcome_counts"]` set by `*_decide`'s output. No
   downstream INDETERMINATE/PASS logic changes — this is additive observability, exactly like
   the drop-in for `verification_contract_violations` above.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `plan_review_decide` and `code_review_decide` both emit an `outcome_counts` field (a
      single-run `{clean, recovered, empty_outcomes, unrecoverable}` tally, `unrecoverable`
      always `0` per the documented boundary limitation above) computed by ONE shared
      `review_kernel` helper — no duplicated tallying logic between the two pipelines.
- [ ] `code_review_decide` records contract violations via the SAME shared
      `collect_contract_violations`/`record_contract_violation`/`drain_contract_violations`
      sink `plan_review_decide` uses (moved into `review_kernel`), activated in
      `gate_dispatch._run_code_review_gate` and drained into
      `finalize_code_review_verdict`'s `coverage["verification_contract_violations"]`.
- [ ] Existing plan-review and code-review test suites continue to pass unmodified (the sink
      move is a mechanical relocation — plan-review's behavior is byte-identical); new tests
      cover code-review's `outcome_counts`/contract-violation propagation specifically, and at
      least one test covers the moved sink from its new shared location for both pipelines.
- [ ] No change to either pipeline's decision thresholds, impact functions, or verdict shape
      (`outcome_counts` and `verification_contract_violations` are additive coverage keys only).

## Verify commands

- `pytest tests/unit/test_review_kernel.py tests/unit/test_review_kernel_rules.py -q`
- `pytest tests/unit/plan_review -q`
- `pytest tests/unit/code_review -q`
- `pytest tests -q`
compiled_state.title: Wire shared verify_findings outcome_counts telemetry into plan_review and code_review consumer pipelines
